findings.evidence: variance() calls mean(values) without catching exceptions. Before this PR, variance([]) raised ZeroDivisionError (propagated from mean). After this PR, variance([]) raises ValueError("empty dataset") instead. The PR declares the error-contract change only for mean(); the same change silently applies to variance() as a side-effect.
findings.why_tests_missed_it: No test exercises variance([]) in either base or head; the changed error type for variance on empty input is never observed by the test suite.
findings.fix: Document in variance()'s docstring that it raises ValueError on empty input (mirroring mean), or add a test for variance([]) so the changed error contract is explicit and locked down.
